data.title: China's top food delivery firm Meituan's revenue in line with estimates
data.description:
China's largest food delivery firm Meituan posted on Friday a fourth-quarter revenue that was largely in line with estimates, amid sluggish Chinese consumption.

In the quarter, Meituan's revenue reached 88.5 billion yuan, just above analysts' estimates of 87.7 billion yuan, according to LSEG data.

Meituan, whose app offers a wide range of services including bike-sharing, ticket-booking, and maps, reported a revenue of 337.59 billion yuan for the year, up from 276.75 billion yuan the previous year.

Its net profit for the 12-month period reached 35.81 billion yuan, compared to a profit of 13.86 billion yuan a year earlier.

Amid cost-conscious Chinese consumers, low-cost and discounted products have become a focus for both shoppers and platforms, which has benefited Meituan as it delivers these goods for a small fee.

data.title: ANE (Cayman)'s 2024 Profit Rises to 750 Million Yuan
data.description:
ANE (Cayman) recorded a rise in attributable profit for 2024 to 749.7 million yuan from 392.4 million yuan a year prior, a Thursday filing with the Hong Kong bourse said.

The express freight network operator's earnings per share climbed to 0.65 yuan from 0.34 yuan in the previous fiscal year.

Revenue rose 17% year over year to 11.6 billion yuan from 9.92 billion yuan, mainly due to a higher freight volume for the year alongside a stable unit price for total service.

data.title: High density polyethylene (HDPE) prices show mixed trend in Europe
data.description: This week, HDPE prices displayed a mixed undertone in the European region.An industry source in Europe reported a Polymerupdate team member, " The European high-density polyethylene (HDPE) spot market continues to experience downward pressure due to weak demand and limited trading activity. A lack of buyer interest and cautious seller offers have further contributed to the subdued market environment. A market participant commented that purchasing has significantly declined, resulting in a disappointing month for both volumes and inquiries, leading to operations falling short of budget expectations. The supply fundamentals for HDPE remain mixed, with varying market dynamics across different grades. Another participant noted that HDPE is currently one of the most challenging products, underscoring the ongoing difficulties in the market. Additionally, the halt of imports from the US has intensified market volatility and uncertainty. Overall, demand remains highly unpredictable, compounding the challenges faced by the market. Market participants were surprised by the European Commission's proposal to impose tariffs on polyethylene (PE) imports from the US. This action is a response to the US's implementation of 25% tariffs on steel, aluminum, and related products from the EU and other nations. Consultations regarding these reciprocal tariffs have commenced, and if approved by EU member states, they are set to take effect from April 13. Although the specific tariff rate on PE and other products has not been revealed, a senior official suggested it could reach as high as 25%. This situation has introduced additional uncertainty into PE pricing dynamics, leading many market participants to adopt a cautious approach as they evaluate the potential consequences. Furthermore, supply-side implications may arise, as sellers managing US-origin imports reassess their strategies, including decisions regarding shipments already en route to Europe.In spot markets, HDPE BM grade prices were assessed at the Euro 1155-1165/mt FD North West Europe levels, while HDPE injection grade prices were assessed at the Euro 1125-1135/mt FD North West Europe levels, both falling by Euro (-10/mt) from the previous week. Meanwhile, HDPE injection grade prices were assessed at the Euro 1165-1175/mt FD North West Europe levels, steady from week on week.In the contract markets, HDPE film grade prices were assessed at the Euro 1630-1635/mt FD NWE Germany and FD NWE Italy levels, both rising by Euro (+10/mt) from last week. HDPE film grade prices were assessed at the Euro 1630-1635/mt FD NWE France levels, a gain of Euro (+10/mt) from week on week. Meanwhile, HDPE film grade prices were assessed at the GBP 1365-1370/mt FD NWE UK levels, a marginal rise of GBP (+5/mt) from the previous week.In the contract markets, HDPE BM grade prices were assessed at the Euro 1610-1615/mt FD NWE Germany and FD NWE Italy levels, both gaining by Euro (+10/mt) week on week. HDPE BM grade prices were assessed at the Euro 1610-1615/mt FD NWE France levels, a rise of Euro (+10/mt) from the previous week. Meanwhile, HDPE BM grade prices were assessed at the GBP 1350-1355/mt FD NWE UK levels, up GBP (+5/mt) from last week.In the contract markets, HDPE injection grade prices were assessed at the Euro 1570-1575/mt FD NWE Germany and FD NWE Italy levels, both week on week up Euro (+10/mt). HDPE injection grade prices were assessed at the Euro 1570-1575/mt FD NWE France levels, a gain of Euro (+10/mt) from the previous week. Meanwhile, HDPE injection grade prices were assessed at the GBP 1315-1325/mt FD NWE UK levels, up GBP (+5/mt) from last week.The source added, "In the contract market, certain participants noted a retreat from the price hikes anticipated by suppliers, even in light of positive trends in upstream feedstock. Conversely, other market sources presented differing opinions, indicating month-over-month price increases. Throughout the week, market participants kept a vigilant eye on developments concerning possible EU tariffs on imports from the US. The discussions surrounding these trade measures have intensified, raising apprehensions about their potential effects on market dynamics. Uncertainty persists as stakeholders await further information regarding the specifics and extent of the proposed tariffs. Many expect that the confirmation of any tariff implementation could result in a substantial shift in near term market sentiment and trading conditions. FCA Antwerp HDPE film prices were assessed at the Euro 1150-1190/mt levels, steady week on week. FCA Antwerp HDPE BM prices were assessed at the Euro 1140-1170/mt levels, while FCA Antwerp HDPE injection prices were assessed at the Euro 1110-1140/mt levels, both falling by Euro (-10/mt) from last week.Ethylene spot prices on Thursday were assessed at the Euro 850-860/mt FD North West Europe levels, sharply higher by Euro (-50/mt) from the previous week. European Eehylene contract price for March 2025 settled at the Euro 1260/MT FD North West Europe levels. This price represents a marginal gain of Euro 2.50/MT from its February 2025 settlement levels.

data.title: Benzene prices remain steady in Europe
data.description: On Thursday, benzene prices were left unchanged in Europe.An industry source in Europe on condition of anonymity informed a Polymerupdate team member, "Prices remained stable as the region faced ample supply amid sluggish demand from the derivative sectors."FOB Rotterdam benzene prices were assessed at the USD 735-745/mt levels, stable from Wednesday.Downstream SM prices on Thursday were assessed at the USD 1210-1220/mt FOB Rotterdam levels, a day on day gain of USD (+5/mt).Meanwhile in Asia, benzene prices on Thursday were assessed at the USD 820-830/mt FOB Korea levels, a rise of USD (+10/mt) from Wednesday's assessed levels.

data.title: Baozun Returns to Profit in Q4'24
data.description:
Baozun returned to an attributable loss narrowed to about 129,000 yuan in the fourth quarter of 2024 from 48.4 million yuan in the year-ago period, according to a Thursday filing with the Hong Kong bourse.

The integrated brand e-commerce company returned to earnings per share of less than 0.01 yuan from a loss per share of 0.27 yuan in the previous year.

Revenue grew 7.7% to 2.99 billion yuan from 2.78 billion a year earlier.

data.title: Global Markets Fell Over Growth Fears on Continued Tit-For-Tat Tariffs
data.description:
By Dow Jones Newswires Staff

Major global stock markets were down Friday, tracking Wall Street, after a week of central bank action and as tit-for-tat tariffs continued to raise questions over growth.

Safe havens, in particular the dollar and gold, continue to benefit from the uncertainty. In Europe, equities and the euro remained under pressure after European Central Bank President Christine Lagarde warned on Thursday that tariffs would weaken growth and fuel inflation.

In a key event of the day, the Bundesrat--Germany's legislative body that represents the 16 German states--is set to vote later on the country's fiscal expansion, with overwhelming expectations that the vote will pass. The German 10-year Bund yield fell by three basis points in early trade.

  • U.S. futures for the S&P 500 were down 0.3% and futures for the Dow Jones Industrial Average declined 0.3%. In Europe, the Stoxx Europe 600 dropped 0.8%. Shares in Puma and Adidas fell after U.S. sporting-goods giant Nike said Thursday that it expects its sales drop to accelerate. The FTSE 100 lost 0.4%. The CAC 40 and DAX were down around 0.5% and 0.8% respectively.
  • The dollar rose as general risk aversion drove investors to safe havens. The DXY dollar index was last up 0.2% against a basket of major currencies to 104.001 after hitting a two-week high of 104.147 earlier.
  • Gold futures eased a touch, down 0.2% at $3,038.90 a troy ounce, but were holding near the new record high of $3,065.20/oz set on Thursday. Gold is up 14% year-to-date and 1.6% on week.
  • U.S. 10-year Treasury yields edged slightly higher, after falling this week. The rally in U.S. Treasurys this week suggests that markets have taken note of the slowing growth narrative, strategists at Barclays Research said in a note. The 10-year Treasury yield was up 1.5 basis points to 4.248%.
  • Asian shares were mixed with Japanese and Chinese stocks following the weakness on Wall Street. The Nikkei closed 0.2% lower, and JGB bond yields gained half a basis point to 1.520%. In mainland China, shares continued to extend their losses, with some profit-taking after a recent rally.
  • Bitcoin was down 0.2% at $83,960.28.

Write to Barcelona Editors at barcelonaeditors@dowjones.com

data.title: Teeyer Intelligent Equipment Ties Up With German Mechanical Engineer Company to Set Up EUR1 Million JV
data.description:
Jiangsu Teeyer Intelligent Equipment partnered with German mechanical engineer company Wehrhahn to establish a 1 million euro joint venture in Jiangsu Province, China, according to a Shanghai Stock Exchange disclosure on Thursday.

The JV, Tianyuan Wehrhahn Smart Technology, will offer technical services and produce mechanical equipment and industrial automatic control systems, as well as other electromechanical components.

The building materials equipment maker will invest 600,000 euros into the venture's capital and Wehrhahn will contribute 400,000 euros, owning 60% and 40% of the venture, respectively.

Teeyer's shares closed over 1% higher.

data.title: Meituan Nearly Triples Profit Despite Competition
data.description:
By Kimberley Kao

Chinese food-delivery giant Meituan reported a near tripling of fourth-quarter profit as revenue jumped, continuing its run of strong earnings even as it faces fierce competition and a weaker Chinese economy.

The Beijing-based shopping-and-delivery platform on Friday said fourth-quarter net profit was 6.22 billion yuan, equivalent to about $858 million, up from 2.22 billion yuan a year earlier. The result was lower than the 7.94 billion yuan expected of analysts polled by Visible Alpha.

Revenue rose 20% to 88.49 billion yuan, beating a Visible Alpha estimate of 87.77 billion yuan.

Quarterly revenue from the core local commerce segment rose 19% to 65.57 billion yuan, partly due to further online penetration and strong consumer demand, it said.

The company's new initiatives segment grew its revenue by 23%, while operating loss for the segment roughly halved to 2.18 billion yuan.

"We will expand our investments in cutting-edge technologies and the relevant applications, such as AI, unmanned aerial delivery, and autonomous delivery vehicles," the company said. "In the long run, we are convinced that technology will continue to transform the retail industry."

Meituan, a dominant player in the Chinese food-delivery market, has been facing stiff competition in its home market from the likes of Ele.me, the food-delivery service owned by Alibaba Group, and ByteDance's Douyin, TikTok's sister platform in China. It has been looking to expand overseas to bolster growth as its domestic business slows, with international platform Keeta gaining ground in Hong Kong and Saudi Arabia.

Investors' optimism about Meituan's growth prospects has propelled its shares. The company's Hong Kong-listed stock rose 85% in 2024 and is up more than 10% so far this year, though it remains far from the all-time high reached in early 2021.

Write to Kimberley Kao at kimberley.kao@wsj.com

data.title: N. Macedonia's mobile phone subscribers flattish y/y in 2024
data.description:
SKOPJE (N. Macedonia), March 21 (SeeNews) - The number of mobile phone subscribers in North Macedonia edged down 0.04% year-on-year to 1.92 million at the end of 2024, data from the Agency for Electronic Communications (AEK) showed.

North Macedonia has six mobile operators together serving a market of 1.8 million people.

At the end of 2024, A1 Makedonija, part of A1 Telekom Austria Group, had a 47.90% share of the overall number of mobile subscribers, followed by Makedonski Telekom, part of Deutsche Telekom, with a 46.57% share, AEK said in an annual statement last week.

Lycamobile had 3.05%, Telekabel 1.29%, Mtel 1.08%, and Green Mobile 0.11%. Mtel, part of Telekom Srbija, acquired the local unit of international mobile virtual network operator (MVNO) Lycamobile in late 2024.

The number of fixed-line subscribers in North Macedonia increased 1.96% on the year to 449,868 in 2024. The number of fixed broadband internet users went up 3.13% to 538,254 last year, while mobile internet users increased 4.07% to 1.5 million.

data.title: Shinva Medical Instrument's Subsidiary Gets Registration Certificate for 3D Fluorescence Endoscope Image Processor
data.description:
Shinva Medical Instrument's subsidiary, Xinhua Surgical Instruments, received a medical device registration certificate from the Chinese drug administration for its 3D fluorescence endoscope image processor.

The device is used in conjunction with the company's 3D fluorescence electronic endoscope to provide visible light and near-infrared fluorescence images during endoscopic surgery, according to a Shanghai Stock Exchange filing on Thursday.

The certificate is valid until March 9, 2030, the Chinese medical device and pharmaceutical machinery manufacturer said.

The company's shares closed over 1% lower.

data.title: Mandatory notification of trade for closely associated company of PDMR
data.description:
AHB Invest AS, a closely associated company of Chairman of the Board ofDirectors Arild Bødal, has on 21 March 2025 sold 22 000 equity capitalcertificates in Sparebanken Vest at a price of NOK 148.5. The total holding ofequity capital certificates for AHB Invest AS is after this 49,001 equitycapital certificates.

Any questions may be directed to Director of Finance, Operations and InvestorRelations, Brede Borgen Kristiansen on +47 479 06 402.

data.title: Decent Land Labs protocol for onchain data center, Load Network Breaks EVM Storage Barrier with 492GB transaction
data.description:
LINCOLN, England, March 21, 2025 (GLOBE NEWSWIRE) — Load Network, formerly known as WeaveVM, has announced the largest known EVM transaction size limit of 492GB, pivoting from an EVM compatibility layer for Arweave storage into the “first onchain data center”. With transaction size limits lifted, Load Network can store 40% of Ethereum’s ledger history or 300 hours of HD video in a single EVM base layer transaction.

From WeaveVM to Load Network: A Mission Evolved

Launched in late 2023, WeaveVM set out to bridge Arweave’s permanent storage with the EVM ecosystem. Since 2024, it has become the largest data protocol on Arweave by monthly transaction volume, processing approximately 5 million transactions per month.

Serving as storage for prominent chains like Avalanche, Metis, and RSS3, and data availability for Dymension, the network has outgrown its original scope. Rebranded as Load Network, it now delivers the storage, high-bandwidth compute, and seamless integration at a fraction of traditional costs—positioning itself as a drop-in “cloud service” for blockchains, data availability (DA) layers, and dApps.

Breaking the EVM Storage Barrier

Load Network’s new transaction format obliterates the kilobyte-scale limits of traditional EVM chains, enabling bundles up to 492GB. For context, a single Load transaction can encapsulate a complete Llama 3 70B AI model, hundreds of hours of HD video, or data equivalent to several days of Celestia’s throughput. Currently processing 40% more transactions than Celestia with 700 times the bandwidth, Load Network brings data center-scale capabilities to the blockchain. This advancement makes onchain storage viable for AI models, media, research datasets, and analytics.

“EVM storage has typically been so slow and expensive it’s pushed developers to offchain solutions. When the data and compute don’t live side by side, there are harsh limits on the application feature set. The perception that the EVM is a simple decentralized calculator is wrong,” says Decent Land Labs co-founder and CEO Benjamin Brandall, adding that “so far the storage bottleneck has limited the scope for what a dApp, chain, or data availability layer can really do.”

Why Onchain Data Matters

Most EVM-based dApps rely on centralized Web2 services like AWS for data storage due to cost and capacity constraints, introducing centralization risks and vulnerabilities akin to the Bybit hack. Load Network eliminates this dependency by natively integrating storage and compute, making data accessible within smart contracts. This empowers automation—such as decentralized agents—by ensuring data is verifiable and onchain, not siloed behind centralized endpoints.

The Load AVS Layer: Ethereum-Secured Data Guarantees

Load Network is expanding with an EigenLayer-powered “hot cache” AVS layer, bridging the EVM front end and Arweave’s permanent “cold storage.” Capable of over 800x the combined egress of all rollups tracked by rollup.wtf, this layer ensures data availability with Ethereum-grade security. It also lays the foundation for a decentralized marketplace of bundling services, incentivizing operators to upload, cache, and serve data—further decentralizing the network’s infrastructure.

About Load Network

Load Network is a EVM-compatible Layer 1 blockchain built for storage and high-throughput data availability. Through protocol-level integrations with Arweave and EigenLayer, it offers a performant, flexible and scalable data layer for EVM dApps, protocols and chains.

For more information about Load Network, visit load.network or contact:

Benjamin Brandall

Co-founder & CEO

Decent Land Labs

ben@decent.land

A photo accompanying this announcement is available at https://www.globenewswire.com/NewsRoom/AttachmentNg/215bda29-c18c-402b-a677-e8173697c922
